headline: SVAR IRF estimates are exposed in full so you can read the result and reproduce it manually.

sections.title: What is shown on this page
items: Figures included: SVAR IRF for return_a, Cumulative SVAR IRF for return_a.

sections.title: How to read the main result
items: The recursive identification is driven by the Cholesky ordering: return_a, return_b, return_c.
Shock variable: return_a. Response panels and the IRF table should match horizon by horizon.
Use the cumulative IRF figure when you care about accumulated rather than one-period responses.

sections.title: What a normal paper should report
items: A cumulative IRF figure or cumulative response table when accumulation matters.
A clear statement of ordering or other identification assumptions.

sections.title: Manual replication focus
items: Do not change the variable ordering when reproducing the result.
Rebuild the orthogonalized IRF using the same lag order and horizon.
Compare the cumulative IRF figure against the cumulative response column in the IRF table.
